title: कुबेर लक्ष्मी स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री कुबेर लक्ष्मी स्तोत्रम् ॥

कुबेरं धनदं वन्दे लक्ष्मीं च धनदायिनीम्।
उभौ सम्पत्प्रदातारौ भक्तानां कामपूरकौ॥ १॥

धनाध्यक्षं यक्षराजं कुबेरमभिपूजयेत्।
लक्ष्मीं श्रियं च सम्पूज्य निधिद्वयमवाप्नुयात्॥ २॥

ॐ यक्षाय कुबेराय वैश्रवणाय
धनधान्यपतये धनधान्यसमृद्धिं मे
देहि दापय स्वाहा॥ ३॥

ॐ श्रीं ह्रीं क्लीं महालक्ष्मी
धनदायिनि सर्वसंपत्प्रदे।
कुबेरसमेते लक्ष्मि
धनमागच्छ मम गृहे स्वाहा॥ ४॥

कुबेरो लक्ष्मीसहितो मम गृहे सदा वसेत्।
नवनिधीश्वरं धीमन्तं पद्मशंखनिधिप्रदम्॥ ५॥

पद्मनिधिं शंखनिधिं कुबेरं समुपास्महे।
महालक्ष्मीं धनलक्ष्मीं श्रियं वन्दामहे सदा॥ ६॥

ॐ कुबेराय नमः। ॐ महालक्ष्म्यै नमः।
धनमागच्छ धनमागच्छ धनमागच्छ मम गृहे।
सुवर्णं रजतं धान्यं रत्नानि विविधानि च।
कुबेरलक्ष्मीकृपया सर्वं मे संभवत्विह॥ ७॥

कुबेरो लोकपालश्च लक्ष्मीर्विष्णोः प्रिया सती।
उभयोः कृपया पूर्णं भवतु मम जीवनम्॥ ८॥

न दारिद्र्यं न दैन्यं च न ऋणं न च संकटम्।
कुबेरलक्ष्मीस्तोत्रेण सर्वं शुभमवाप्नुयात्॥ ९॥

इदं कुबेरलक्ष्मीस्तोत्रं यः पठेद्धनलाभकृत्।
त्रिसन्ध्यं वा एकसन्ध्यं दारिद्र्यं तस्य नश्यति॥ १०॥

॥ इति श्री कुबेर लक्ष्मी स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Kubera Lakshmi Stotram ||

Kuberam Dhanadam vande Lakshmim cha dhana-dayinim |
Ubhau sampat-pradatarau bhaktanam kama-purakau || 1 ||

Dhanadhyaksham Yaksha-rajam Kuberam-abhipujayet |
Lakshmim shriyam cha sampujya nidhi-dvayam-avapnuyat || 2 ||

Om Yakshaya Kuberaya Vaishravanaya
Dhana-dhanya-pataye dhana-dhanya-samriddhim me
Dehi dapaya svaha || 3 ||

Om Shrim Hrim Klim Maha-Lakshmi
Dhana-dayini sarva-sampat-prade |
Kubera-samete Lakshmi
Dhanam-agachchha mama grihe svaha || 4 ||

Kubero Lakshmi-sahitao mama grihe sada vaset |
Nava-nidhishvaram dhimantam Padma-Shankha-nidhi-pradam || 5 ||

Padma-nidhim Shankha-nidhim Kuberam samupasmahe |
Maha-Lakshmim Dhana-Lakshmim shriyam vandamahe sada || 6 ||

Om Kuberaya namah | Om Mahalakshmyai namah |
Dhanam-agachchha dhanam-agachchha dhanam-agachchha mama grihe |
Suvarnam rajatam dhanyam ratnani vividhani cha |
Kubera-Lakshmi-kripaya sarvam me sambhavat-viha || 7 ||

Kubero Loka-palashcha Lakshmiar-Vishnoh priya sati |
Ubhayoh kripaya purnam bhavatu mama jivanam || 8 ||

Na daridryam na dainyam cha na rinam na cha sankatam |
Kubera-Lakshmi-stotrena sarvam shubham-avapnuyat || 9 ||

Idam Kubera-Lakshmi-stotram yah pathed-dhana-labha-krit |
Tri-sandhyam va eka-sandhyam daridryam tasya nashyati || 10 ||

|| Iti Shri Kubera Lakshmi Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
धनद कुबेर और धनदायिनी लक्ष्मी को वन्दन करता हूँ। दोनों सम्पत्ति प्रदान करने वाले और भक्तों की कामना पूरी करने वाले हैं॥ १॥

धन के अध्यक्ष, यक्षराज कुबेर की पूजा करें। लक्ष्मी और श्री की पूजा करके पद्मनिधि और शंखनिधि प्राप्त करें॥ २॥

ॐ यक्ष कुबेर, वैश्रवण, धन-धान्य के पति को, मुझे धन-धान्य समृद्धि दो और दिलाओ, स्वाहा॥ ३॥

ॐ श्रीं ह्रीं क्लीं महालक्ष्मी, धनदायिनी, सर्वसम्पत्ति प्रदान करने वाली, कुबेर सहित लक्ष्मी, मेरे घर में धन आओ, स्वाहा॥ ४॥

कुबेर लक्ष्मी सहित मेरे घर में सदा निवास करें। नव निधियों के ईश्वर, बुद्धिमान, पद्मनिधि और शंखनिधि प्रदान करने वाले॥ ५॥

पद्मनिधि, शंखनिधि, कुबेर की उपासना करते हैं। महालक्ष्मी, धनलक्ष्मी, श्री को सदा वन्दना करते हैं॥ ६॥

ॐ कुबेर को नमः। ॐ महालक्ष्मी को नमः। धन आओ, धन आओ, धन आओ मेरे घर में। सुवर्ण, रजत, धान्य और विविध रत्न - कुबेर-लक्ष्मी की कृपा से मुझे सब प्राप्त हो॥ ७॥

कुबेर लोकपाल हैं और लक्ष्मी विष्णु की प्रिय पत्नी। दोनों की कृपा से मेरा जीवन पूर्ण हो॥ ८॥

न दरिद्रता, न दीनता, न ऋण, न संकट - कुबेर-लक्ष्मी स्तोत्र से सब शुभ प्राप्त होता है॥ ९॥

जो इस धनलाभकारी कुबेर-लक्ष्मी स्तोत्र का त्रिसन्ध्या या एकसन्ध्या पाठ करता है, उसकी दरिद्रता नष्ट होती है॥ १०॥
benefits: Combined prayer to Kubera and Lakshmi for maximum wealth blessings
Traditionally recited for removal of poverty and financial difficulties
Invokes the blessing of padma nidhi and shankha nidhi (divine treasures)
Attracts gold, silver, grains, and precious gems
Traditionally recited for removal of debts and financial distress
Ideal for recitation during Dhanteras and Diwali
description: The Kubera Lakshmi Stotram, known in Sanskrit as कुबेर लक्ष्मी स्तोत्रम्, is a devotional hymn of praise in praise of Lord Kubera. Attributed to Traditional Wealth Worship Literature, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to combined prayer to Kubera and Lakshmi for maximum wealth blessings as well as remove debts and financial distress. Additionally, it is known to bestow Padma Nidhi and Shankha Nidhi (divine treasures). It is especially recommended on Friday (Shukravar) and Thursday (Guruvar) and during Dhanteras and Akshaya Tritiya for maximum spiritual benefit. The text contains 10 verses and takes around 8 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Kubera Lakshmi Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Kubera Lakshmi Stotram and why are both deities invoked together?
seoFaqs.answer: The Kubera Lakshmi Stotram is a Sanskrit hymn that simultaneously invokes Kubera, the yaksha-raja and treasurer of the gods, and Mahalakshmi, the goddess of wealth and prosperity — the divine pair governing the inflow, increase and preservation of wealth in Hindu tradition. Where Lakshmi represents the dynamic shakti of prosperity (chanchala — ever-moving), Kubera represents the stable accumulation and guardianship of treasure (nidhi-pati — lord of the nine treasures). Reciting them together is regarded as creating the complete energetic configuration for wealth attraction and retention. The stotra is preserved in classical Vaishnava tradition and is widely recited on Dhanteras, Diwali Lakshmi Puja and Akshaya Tritiya. (Sources: Padma Purana; Stotra Nidhi Kubera Lakshmi; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Kubera and what is his role in Hindu cosmology?
seoFaqs.answer: Kubera is the lord of the yakshas (nature spirits), king of the Himalayan city of Alaka near Mount Kailash, treasurer of the devas, guardian (dikpala) of the north direction, and master of the nava-nidhi — the nine cosmic treasures (Padma, Mahapadma, Shankha, Makara, Kachchhapa, Mukunda, Nanda, Nila and Kharva). Originally a yaksha chief in Vedic tradition, he was elevated through tapasya pleasing Brahma and Shiva. The Mahabharata, Ramayana (he is half-brother of Ravana) and Puranas describe his Pushpaka vimana, his three legs (one lost in punishment), and his obese pot-bellied iconography symbolising abundance. (Sources: Mahabharata Sabha Parva; Ramayana Uttara Kanda; Hindupedia Kubera.)

seoFaqs.question: How is Lakshmi related to Kubera in the wealth tradition?
seoFaqs.answer: Lakshmi as Mahalakshmi, Dhanya-Lakshmi and Dhana-Lakshmi (two of her Ashta-Lakshmi forms) is the source goddess of all wealth, while Kubera as her appointed treasurer (kosha-adhikari) holds and distributes that wealth on her command. Tradition holds that without Lakshmi's grace, even the treasures Kubera guards cannot reach a devotee, while without Kubera's stewardship, Lakshmi's blessings flow out as quickly as they come. The Sri Sukta of the Rigveda invokes both — Lakshmi as the source and Kubera (referenced via his epithet Vaishravana) as the manager. Worshipping them as a pair therefore secures both inflow and retention of prosperity. (Sources: Rigveda Sri Sukta; Padma Purana; Hindupedia Ashta-Lakshmi.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Kubera Lakshmi Stotram be recited for wealth attraction?
seoFaqs.answer: The most powerful occasions are Dhanteras (Kartika Krishna Trayodashi, two days before Diwali, the day specifically of Kubera-Dhanvantari worship and gold purchase), Diwali Lakshmi Puja (Kartika Amavasya), Akshaya Tritiya (Vaishakha Shukla Tritiya — the never-decreasing day, ideal for new ventures), every Friday (Lakshmi's day), Pushya nakshatra days, Sundays of Shravana, and the Margashirsha month. Daily recitation in brahma-muhurta facing north (Kubera's direction) or east, with a yellow asana and offerings of yellow flowers, kheer, and gold-foil, is the prescribed sadhana. (Sources: Drikpanchang Dhanteras; Hindupedia Lakshmi Vrata; Mahalakshmi Sadhana tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the prescribed puja vidhi for Kubera Lakshmi worship?
seoFaqs.answer: The traditional vidhi places a Kubera yantra (or coin/image) in the north of the puja area and a Lakshmi yantra/image facing east, alongside a kalasha filled with water, mango leaves and a coconut. Light a ghee lamp, offer haldi-kumkum, akshata, yellow and red flowers, dhoop, deepa, naivedya (kheer, sweets, fruits, makhana), and chant the Sankalpa. Recite the Ganesha invocation, then the Sri Sukta, then the Kubera Lakshmi Stotram eleven, twenty-one or one hundred and eight times, followed by Mahalakshmi Ashtakam and Kubera Ashtottara. Conclude with aarti (Om Jai Lakshmi Mata), pradakshina and prasad distribution. Place a few coins under the Kubera image overnight. (Sources: Hindupedia Lakshmi puja; Drikpanchang; Stotra Nidhi vidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: Which directions and yantras are associated with Kubera and Lakshmi?
seoFaqs.answer: Kubera is the dikpala of the north (uttara), so the Kubera Yantra is traditionally placed on a north wall or in the north zone of the home or business, often accompanied by a money-locker or cash-box in the north (the bedroom's south-west, called the Kubera-sthana in Vastu, holds the heavy almirah while the actual yantra faces north). Lakshmi yantras include the Sri Yantra (her supreme abstract form), the Mahalakshmi Yantra and the Dhana-Lakshmi Yantra; these are typically placed east-facing or on the puja altar. The combination of north-facing Kubera and east-facing Lakshmi is regarded in Vastu as the optimal wealth configuration. (Sources: Vastu Shastra texts; Hindupedia Yantra; Drikpanchang Kubera direction.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Kubera Lakshmi Stotram promise to its reciters?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition holds that regular recitation grants the steady increase of wealth (dhana-vriddhi), success in business ventures and trade (vyapara-vriddhi), removal of debts (rina-mochana), recovery of lost or stolen property, expansion of agricultural yield and inheritance, harmony in family wealth matters, protection of accumulated assets from theft and natural disasters, and the cultivation of generous wealth-stewardship (dana). For households facing chronic financial drainage, the stotra is recited daily through one mandala of forty days. Beyond material prosperity, it cultivates the spiritual quality of aparigraha (non-grasping) and shradhdha-dhana (the wealth of faith). (Sources: Padma Purana phalashruti; Hindupedia Lakshmi-Kubera; Stotra Nidhi.)
